title: Prepaid BIN Sequential Test-and-Exploit Attack with Low-Value Priming
narrative: Fraudster acquires a full prepaid BIN range (typically 416xxx, 438xxx, or 486xxx IINs with low issuer oversight) and sequentially tests cards with $1.00-$4.99 authorizations to identify active accounts, then immediately escalates approved cards to $85-$175 digital goods purchases within 3-8 minutes. The prepaid nature means no cardholder statements, delayed fraud detection, and minimal AVS/address enforcement. Attack runs 02:00-06:00 local time targeting gaming (ProductCD=C) and streaming subscriptions. Fraudster uses residential proxies with consistent P_emaildomain (temp-mail services like guerrillamail, tempmail.com) across the BIN range. Successful cards show card1_txn_count_1hr jumping from 1 to 3-5, with TransactionAmt z-score spiking above 2.8 on second transaction. The prepaid issuer's low decline rate (often 85%+ approval on valid cards) enables rapid enumeration before risk systems adapt.
indicators: Sequential card1 values within same BIN6, incrementing by 1-10 across 15-40 transactions in 20-90 minute window
Two-phase amount pattern: initial $1.00-$4.99 test, then $85-$175 escalation within 3-8 minutes for approved cards
card1_txn_count_1hr increases from 1 to 3-5 for same card1 value within 10-minute window
Prepaid BIN range (416xxx, 438xxx, 486xxx) with approval rate >80% and ProductCD=C or W
Consistent P_emaildomain from disposable services (guerrillamail, tempmail, mailinator) across 70%+ of transactions in sequence
TransactionAmt z-score jump from <0.5 to >2.8 between first and second transaction on same card1
txn_hour concentrated in 2-6 range with is_night=1, dist1 values indicating VPN/proxy (null or >2000km)
summary: Sequential card numbers from the same prepaid BIN are tested with small $1-5 charges, then immediately escalated to $85-175 purchases within minutes if approved, all using disposable email addresses and occurring overnight through proxies.
reasoning: The fraudster is systematically probing a stolen BIN range to find active prepaid cards that lack statement oversight, then rapidly draining approved cards before detection systems catch up or cardholders notice unauthorized charges.
indicator_explanations: Attacker is testing consecutive card numbers from the same issuer block
Small test charges confirm valid cards before immediately hitting them harder
Same card suddenly fires multiple transactions after sitting dormant
Prepaid cards from known BIN ranges show suspicious approval clustering
Throwaway email services repeat across the attack sequence
Transaction amount spikes dramatically after the initial test succeeds
Activity clusters in overnight hours from VPN or geographically impossible locations
